trans-unit.source: 🚧 This section of the training platform and the entire content is under ⚠️construction⚠️ and may not be shared or published! 🚧
trans-unit.target: 🚧 This section of the training platform and the entire content is under ⚠️construction⚠️ and may not be shared or published! 🚧

trans-unit.source: (content:references:module9:network-theory)=
trans-unit.target: (content:references:module9:network-theory)=

trans-unit.source: Spatial Network Analysis Theory
trans-unit.target: Spatial Network Analysis Theory

trans-unit.source: Understanding Networks: Analysis and Insights
trans-unit.target: Understanding Networks: Analysis and Insights

trans-unit.source: Networks serve as simplified representations of real-world systems. A network comprises nodes and edges, representing entities and their relationships respectively. Rather than being defined by coordinates, positions within a network are determined by connectivity. In GIS (Geographic Information Systems), networks play integral roles in transportation modeling and supply chain networks such as water and energy distribution. Additionally, they are utilized for analyzing the service areas of healthcare facilities. For instance, analyses can assess areas vulnerable to flooding to ascertain the potential impact on healthcare services during a disaster.
trans-unit.target: Networks serve as simplified representations of real-world systems. A network comprises nodes and edges, representing entities and their relationships respectively. Rather than being defined by coordinates, positions within a network are determined by connectivity. In GIS (Geographic Information Systems), networks play integral roles in transportation modeling and supply chain networks such as water and energy distribution. Additionally, they are utilized for analyzing the service areas of healthcare facilities. For instance, analyses can assess areas vulnerable to flooding to ascertain the potential impact on healthcare services during a disaster.

trans-unit.source: Components of a Graph
trans-unit.target: Components of a Graph

trans-unit.source: Creating a graph involves defining its nodes (vertices) and edges (connections between nodes).
trans-unit.target: Creating a graph involves defining its nodes (vertices) and edges (connections between nodes).

trans-unit.source: **Defining Nodes:** entities, objects, locations **→** Features in OpenStreetMap (hospitals, schools etc.)
trans-unit.target: **Defining Nodes:** entities, objects, locations **→** Features in OpenStreetMap (hospitals, schools etc.)

trans-unit.source: **Identifying Edges:** roads or pathways between locations **→** Ways in OpenStreetMap (roads, paths, cycleways)
trans-unit.target: **Identifying Edges:** roads or pathways between locations **→** Ways in OpenStreetMap (roads, paths, cycleways)

trans-unit.source: **Additional Information** Edges can be endowed with diverse weights. OpenStreetMap features tags such as road type, segment length, speed limit, and surface condition, which can be interpreted as weight attributes. Depending on your requirements, you can compute the shortest route or opt for the route with minimal elevation gain.
trans-unit.target: **Additional Information** Edges can be endowed with diverse weights. OpenStreetMap features tags such as road type, segment length, speed limit, and surface condition, which can be interpreted as weight attributes. Depending on your requirements, you can compute the shortest route or opt for the route with minimal elevation gain.

trans-unit.source: Dijkstra Algorithm
trans-unit.target: Dijkstra Algorithm

trans-unit.source: E. W. Dijkstra
trans-unit.target: E. W. Dijkstra

trans-unit.source: Discovered 1956, published 1959
trans-unit.target: Discovered 1956, published 1959

trans-unit.source: Single pair shortest path algorithm (SPSP) - for directions / A → B routing
trans-unit.target: Single pair shortest path algorithm (SPSP) - for directions / A → B routing

trans-unit.source: Single source shortest path algorithm (SSSP) - for isochrones
trans-unit.target: Single source shortest path algorithm (SSSP) - for isochrones

trans-unit.source: Base of a multitude of routing algorithms
trans-unit.target: Base of a multitude of routing algorithms

trans-unit.source: Analysis
trans-unit.target: Analysis

trans-unit.source: You can determine directions or standard routes, which unveil the shortest path between two points. Isochrones offer insight into the area accessible within a specified time or distance threshold. Additionally, generating a matrix enables the assessment of time or distance between predefined locations. Alternatively, graphs facilitate travel optimization by computing the most efficient sequence for visiting a given set of points.
trans-unit.target: You can determine directions or standard routes, which unveil the shortest path between two points. Isochrones offer insight into the area accessible within a specified time or distance threshold. Additionally, generating a matrix enables the assessment of time or distance between predefined locations. Alternatively, graphs facilitate travel optimization by computing the most efficient sequence for visiting a given set of points.

trans-unit.source: Openrouteservice
trans-unit.target: Openrouteservice

trans-unit.source: General Information
trans-unit.target: General Information

trans-unit.source: Graphhopper based routing machine since 2008
trans-unit.target: Graphhopper based routing machine since 2008

trans-unit.source: Completely Open-Source
trans-unit.target: Completely Open-Source

trans-unit.source: Global public API
trans-unit.target: Global public API

trans-unit.source: updates every week
trans-unit.target: updates every week

trans-unit.source: more than 100GB RAM per routing profile
trans-unit.target: more than 100GB RAM per routing profile

trans-unit.source: some limitations due to computational capacities
trans-unit.target: some limitations due to computational capacities

trans-unit.source: Can also run on your local laptop
trans-unit.target: Can also run on your local laptop

trans-unit.source: a small to medium country with one profile
trans-unit.target: a small to medium country with one profile

trans-unit.source: freedom of choice on limitations
trans-unit.target: freedom of choice on limitations

trans-unit.source: Different routing profiles (car, hgv, bike, pedestrian, wheelchair)
trans-unit.target: Different routing profiles (car, hgv, bike, pedestrian, wheelchair)

trans-unit.source: Services
trans-unit.target: Services

trans-unit.source: **Directions/standard routing**: Shortest path between two locations
trans-unit.target: **Directions/standard routing**: Shortest path between two locations

trans-unit.source: **Isochrones**: Area reachable within a limit (time | distance)
trans-unit.target: **Isochrones**: Area reachable within a limit (time | distance)

trans-unit.source: **Matrix**: Times / Distances between a set of locations
trans-unit.target: **Matrix**: Times / Distances between a set of locations

trans-unit.source: **Optimization**: Best order to visit a set of points
trans-unit.target: **Optimization**: Best order to visit a set of points

trans-unit.source: This module provides an accessibility analysis based on Openrouteservice isochrones.
trans-unit.target: This module provides an accessibility analysis based on Openrouteservice isochrones.

trans-unit.source: Isochrones
trans-unit.target: Isochrones

trans-unit.source: Different profiles: car, pedestrian, bike, heavy goods vehicles (HGV)
trans-unit.target: Different profiles: car, pedestrian, bike, heavy goods vehicles (HGV)

trans-unit.source: Avoid areas, avoid, road types
trans-unit.target: Avoid areas, avoid, road types

trans-unit.source: Further dynamic preferences: green routing, noise aware routing, landmark routing, (heat aware routing)
trans-unit.target: Further dynamic preferences: green routing, noise aware routing, landmark routing, (heat aware routing)

trans-unit.source: Based on profile and preferences what area is reachable within a given time limit?
trans-unit.target: Based on profile and preferences what area is reachable within a given time limit?

trans-unit.source: Determine: reachability, catchment areas
trans-unit.target: Determine: reachability, catchment areas

trans-unit.source: Max. 60minutes on the public API
trans-unit.target: Max. 60minutes on the public API

trans-unit.source: SDKs & Plugins: Python, R, JavaScript, QGIS Plugin "ORS Tools"
trans-unit.target: SDKs & Plugins: Python, R, JavaScript, QGIS Plugin "ORS Tools"

trans-unit.source: For more information click here: [https://openrouteservice.org/]
trans-unit.target: For more information click here: [https://openrouteservice.org/]
